---

# TypeScript

timber.js generates route types at build time. Links, params, and search params are type-checked against the actual route tree.

## Generated Route Map

The build system scans your `app/` directory and generates a `Routes` interface:

```ts
// Generated — do not edit
declare module '@timber/app' {
  interface Routes {
    '/': {};
    '/about': {};
    '/products/[id]': { id: string };
    '/blog/[...slug]': { slug: string[] };
  }
}
```

This powers type-checking across the framework.

## Typed `<Link>`

`<Link>` validates `href` against the route map:

```tsx
import { Link } from '@timber/app/client';

<Link href="/about">About</Link>           // ✓
<Link href="/products/123">Product</Link>   // ✓
<Link href="/nonexistent">Bad</Link>        // ✗ TypeScript error
```

For dynamic routes, use `interpolateParams`:

```tsx
import { Link, interpolateParams } from '@timber/app/client';

<Link href={interpolateParams('/products/[id]', { id: product.id })}>
  {product.name}
</Link>
```

## Typed Params

Page components receive typed params matching the route definition:

```tsx
// app/products/[id]/page.tsx
export default async function ProductPage({
  params,
}: {
  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;
}) {
  const { id } = await params;
  // id is typed as string
}
```

## Typed Search Params

When a `search-params.ts` file exists, search params are fully typed:

```ts
// app/products/search-params.ts
export const searchParams = createSearchParams({
  page: z.coerce.number().default(1),
  sort: z.enum(['price', 'name']).default('price'),
});

// In page.tsx — searchParams() returns { page: number; sort: 'price' | 'name' }
```

## Context Types

### `RouteContext`

Passed to API route handlers (`route.ts`):

```ts
export async function GET(ctx: RouteContext) {
  ctx.params;   // Record<string, string>
  ctx.request;  // Request
  ctx.url;      // URL
}
```

### `MiddlewareContext`

Passed to `middleware.ts`:

```ts
export default async function middleware(ctx: MiddlewareContext) {
  ctx.params;        // Record<string, string>
  ctx.request;       // Request
  ctx.url;           // URL
  ctx.searchParams;  // Typed if search-params.ts exists
}
```

### `AccessContext`

Passed to `access.ts`:

```ts
export default async function access(ctx: AccessContext) {
  ctx.params;        // Record<string, string>
  ctx.searchParams;  // Typed if search-params.ts exists
}
```

## Augmenting Routes

The `Routes` interface is generated by the build system and written to your project. It's module-augmented onto `@timber/app` — you don't need to import it manually.

Run `timber build` or `timber dev` to regenerate types after adding or renaming routes.
